scenario: Stellar Insurance, an insurer ingesting 50000 events per day from a partner system, builds a custom inbound connector for Camunda 8.8. A developer authors the element template and must bind the runtime type io.camunda:mytestinbound:1 so the connector runtime can match incoming subscriptions to the correct executable class at activation time.
question: Which template property binding does the SDK specify for the inbound runtime type value io.camunda:mytestinbound:1?

options.text: A visible String property labelled Type, bound as a zeebe:taskDefinition with property type so the inbound subscription is routed by Zeebe job type at activation time
A Dropdown property bound as a zeebe:input with name inbound.type, where the value io.camunda:mytestinbound:1 appears as one of the editable choices for users in Web Modeler
A Text property bound as a zeebe:taskHeader with key inbound.type, written into the job custom header so the executable can be discovered by the InboundConnectorExecutable SPI at runtime
A Hidden property with value io.camunda:mytestinbound:1, bound as a zeebe:property with name inbound.type so the runtime resolves the executable class from this connection value

a.text: Incorrect. Inbound connectors are not driven by zeebe:taskDefinition; that binding is used for outbound connectors and service tasks, not for inbound subscriptions.

b.text: Incorrect. The type definition is not a user-editable Dropdown bound to zeebe:input; it is a Hidden property carrying a fixed connection value via zeebe:property.

c.text: Incorrect. The binding type is zeebe:property with name inbound.type, not a zeebe:taskHeader key written to the job; inbound discovery does not use task headers for the type.

d.text: Correct. The inbound element template defines a Hidden property whose value is io.camunda:mytestinbound:1 and whose binding is type zeebe:property with name inbound.type. This is the connection that configures which version of the runtime executable to use.

explanation: An inbound connector element template carries the runtime type as a Hidden property with a fixed value and a zeebe:property binding to the name inbound.type. This identifies the executable class version that should handle the subscription, in the same way the outbound binding via zeebe:taskDefinition selects an outbound function.

docs.title: Connector SDK - Inbound connector element template
